intro: Eleocharis palustromicrocarpa, also known as dwarf spike-rush, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Eleocharis palustromicrocarpa include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote relaxation.
